steps.title: Use the zero exponent rule
steps.content: Any nonzero base to the $0$ power equals $1$, so $$(0.1)^0=1$$ Then $$f(0)=320(1)=320$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D, $320$, is correct. $32$ is what you would get at $x=1$, not at $x=0$, because multiplying by $0.1$ happens only after the exponent is 1.
